title: M0: Project skeleton and technical decisions
description: Establish the repository, package boundaries, schema direction, and v0 decision records needed before implementation starts.
acceptance_criteria: CLI skeleton exists; core package boundaries are documented; config schema examples validate; v0 deferred scope is explicitly recorded.

title: Scaffold CLI and package layout
description: Create the initial OSIx CLI and internal package layout for registry, manifests, diffing, crypto, mounts, policy, and agent semantics.
acceptance_criteria: `osix --help` runs; package responsibilities are clear; skeleton builds in CI/local test command.

title: Define OSIx config schema v0.1
description: Turn the config object in the spec into a versioned schema with examples for base, parent, runtime, stateRoots, snapshot, encryption, and integrity fields.
acceptance_criteria: Schema examples in specs validate; required and optional fields are documented; schema versioning rules are stated.

title: Record v0 architecture decisions
description: Write decision records for hybrid publication mode, image-mode fallback, zstd tar diff layers, overlay/fuse-overlayfs mounts, age and AWS KMS encryption, and deferred lazy encrypted reads.
acceptance_criteria: Decision records exist; each decision includes context, choice, tradeoffs, and deferrals.

title: Select local registry test harness
description: Choose and document the local registry setup used for integration tests, including how referrer support and image-mode fallback will be tested.
acceptance_criteria: A local registry can be started by contributors; test harness supports push/pull fixtures; compatibility gaps are documented.

title: M1: OCI manifest and local snapshot prototype
description: Create and restore OSIx snapshots locally without requiring registry push/pull or mounted filesystems.
acceptance_criteria: A fixture directory can be snapshotted into local blobs and restored from an OSIx manifest with config and layer digests preserved.

title: Implement OSIx config encode/decode
description: Implement typed config encoding and decoding for the agent state capsule manifest.
acceptance_criteria: Round-trip tests cover base, parent, runtime, state roots, snapshot metadata, encryption metadata, and integrity metadata.

title: Build OCI manifest writer and reader
description: Create OSIx image manifest construction and parsing with custom media types, config descriptor, layer descriptors, and annotations.
acceptance_criteria: Generated manifests are valid OCI image manifests; unknown annotations round-trip; invalid media types fail validation.

title: Implement local content-addressed blob store
description: Store config and layer blobs by digest on local disk and expose read/write APIs used by snapshot and restore flows.
acceptance_criteria: Blob writes verify digest; duplicate writes are idempotent; missing or corrupt blobs return clear errors.

title: Write zstd tar diff layers from a directory tree
description: Implement v0 layer creation for added and modified files, metadata, symlinks, hardlinks where supported, xattrs where supported, and deletions through OCI whiteouts.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover add, modify, delete, symlink, mode, uid, gid, and reproducible metadata normalization where the platform allows.

title: Restore local snapshots by applying layers
description: Apply OSIx diff layers in order into a target directory, including whiteout deletion handling and integrity checks.
acceptance_criteria: Restoring a local manifest recreates the fixture tree; corrupt layers fail; applying multiple layers preserves final expected state.

title: M2: Registry push and pull
description: Publish and restore OSIx snapshots through standard OCI registry APIs.
acceptance_criteria: `osix snapshot DIR --push --tag snap-000001` publishes to a registry and `osix restore REF DIR` restores from it with digest pinning.

title: Integrate OCI registry client
description: Add registry authentication, reference parsing, manifest resolution, blob existence checks, blob upload, and blob download.
acceptance_criteria: Client can push and pull blobs/manifests from the local registry harness; auth and network errors are actionable.

title: Implement snapshot push flow
description: Push layer blobs, config blobs, and manifests to a registry, then tag the pushed snapshot.
acceptance_criteria: Push is idempotent; missing uploads are retried or fail cleanly; manifest is uploaded only after referenced blobs exist.

title: Implement registry restore flow
description: Resolve a reference, fetch config and layers, verify digests, and restore to a target directory.
acceptance_criteria: Restore by tag records the resolved digest; restore by digest is stable after tags move; missing blobs fail closed.

title: Support image-mode compatibility fallback
description: Publish snapshots as ordinary OCI image manifests when artifact/referrer support is unavailable.
acceptance_criteria: At least one registry works in image mode; client can detect or be configured for fallback; docs explain behavior differences.

title: M3: Mount and diff UX
description: Provide the local filesystem experience: initialize, mount, modify, diff, and snapshot a writable agent filesystem.
acceptance_criteria: Users can run `init`, `mount`, `diff`, and `snapshot` against a writable OSIx filesystem and get only changed files in the snapshot.

title: Create `.osix` local workspace layout
description: Implement local config, cache, upperdir, workdir, manifest cache, and keys directories.
acceptance_criteria: `osix init` creates the documented layout; config records state repo, base image, mount path, branch, encryption, and signing defaults.

title: Implement `osix init`
description: Initialize a state image from a base OCI image and prepare local writable state.
acceptance_criteria: Command resolves and records base digest; creates local layout; handles existing directories safely; prints next-step commands.

title: Implement overlayfs and fuse-overlayfs mount paths
description: Mount base and snapshot lowerdirs with a local writable upperdir using overlayfs when privileged and fuse-overlayfs when rootless.
acceptance_criteria: Writable mount works on supported Linux environments; rootless mode is documented; mount failures explain required permissions or dependencies.

title: Implement `osix diff`
description: Show changed, added, and deleted paths between snapshots or between a mounted upperdir and its parent.
acceptance_criteria: Diff output uses M/A/D markers; supports snapshot-to-snapshot and mount-to-parent; excludes default ignored paths.

title: Snapshot mounted upperdirs
description: Make `osix snapshot ./agentfs` compute the diff from the writable upperdir and optionally push/tag it.
acceptance_criteria: Snapshot emits only changes and whiteouts; `--message`, `--tag`, `--also-tag`, and `--push` behave as specified.

title: M4: Encryption
description: Encrypt snapshot layers before registry upload and restore them only for authorized recipients.
acceptance_criteria: Registry blobs do not contain plaintext layer tar content; restore succeeds with valid keys and fails with invalid keys.

title: Implement per-layer envelope encryption
description: Generate a random DEK for each layer, encrypt the compressed layer, and wrap the DEK for configured recipients.
acceptance_criteria: Ciphertext digest and plaintext digest are tracked; encryption uses authenticated encryption; no plaintext is uploaded when encryption is enabled.

title: Support age recipients
description: Add age recipient parsing, key wrapping, and decrypt support for OSIx encrypted layers.
acceptance_criteria: Snapshot with `--encrypt age:...` restores with the matching identity file and fails with the wrong identity.

title: Support AWS KMS recipients
description: Add AWS KMS key wrapping and decrypt support for OSIx encrypted layers.
acceptance_criteria: Snapshot with `--encrypt kms:aws:kms:...` wraps the DEK; restore uses AWS credentials through the KMS decrypt path; errors are actionable.

title: Audit public encryption metadata
description: Ensure descriptors and annotations expose only minimal approved encryption metadata and never leak path lists or sensitive names.
acceptance_criteria: Public manifest metadata passes a leak review; sensitive indexes live inside encrypted blobs; tests cover disallowed annotation content.

title: M5: Signing and provenance
description: Sign snapshot manifests, attach provenance, and enforce verification policy where configured.
acceptance_criteria: `osix snapshot --sign` attaches a signature; `osix verify REF` validates it; restore can require a trusted signer.

title: Integrate cosign-compatible manifest signing
description: Sign snapshot manifest digests and attach signatures as OCI referrers where supported.
acceptance_criteria: Signed snapshot manifests verify by digest; unsigned or tampered manifests fail verification when policy requires signing.

title: Generate provenance attestations
description: Create provenance payloads containing base digest, parent digest, creator, command version, policy result, signer, and timestamp.
acceptance_criteria: Attestation payload is deterministic enough for tests; required fields are present; payload is attached to the snapshot manifest.

title: Implement `osix verify` and restore-time verification policy
description: Verify signatures and provenance before restore when the local policy requires it.
acceptance_criteria: Trusted signer policy works; missing referrers produce clear fallback behavior; restore fails closed when policy demands verification.

title: M6: Branching, forking, and chain resolution
description: Represent snapshots as a Git-like DAG with branch pointers, parent validation, and stable digest restores.
acceptance_criteria: Forking creates a new branch pointer; restoring by digest remains stable after tags move; unexpected-parent updates report conflicts.

title: Validate parent chains and snapshot sequences
description: Walk parent links, validate base digest consistency, enforce sequence rules, and report broken chains.
acceptance_criteria: Valid chains resolve; broken or missing parents fail clearly; forked DAGs can be traversed.

title: Implement branch tag management
description: Manage mutable branch tags such as `main`, `latest`, `snap-000003`, and feature branches while preserving resolved digests.
acceptance_criteria: Tag updates happen after manifest upload; clients warn on mutable restore references; branch metadata is recorded.

title: Implement `osix fork`
description: Create a new branch pointer from an existing snapshot digest or tag without copying unnecessary blobs.
acceptance_criteria: Fork from snapshot works; fork target can be mounted and snapshotted independently; fork metadata records source digest.

title: Detect expected-parent branch conflicts
description: Detect when a branch head changed between resolution and update and surface a conflict instead of silently moving the branch.
acceptance_criteria: Expected-parent metadata is checked; conflict produces a distinct exit code; docs explain registry-specific CAS limitations.

title: M7: Watcher and turn-boundary snapshots
description: Add periodic, threshold-based, and turn-boundary-aware snapshot automation for running agents.
acceptance_criteria: `osix watch` creates snapshots by interval or dirty byte threshold and can wait for a runtime turn-boundary hook.

title: Implement `osix watch` scheduler
description: Run a local watcher that periodically snapshots a mount and optionally pushes the result.
acceptance_criteria: `--every`, `--push`, and branch/tag options work; failed pushes are retried or surfaced clearly.

title: Track dirty bytes for snapshot thresholds
description: Estimate or measure dirty bytes in a mounted upperdir so watch can snapshot after `--max-dirty`.
acceptance_criteria: Watcher snapshots after threshold; accounting handles deletes and overwrites well enough for policy decisions; docs state precision limits.

title: Add turn-boundary integration hook
description: Define and implement the runtime hook used by `--on-turn-boundary` to avoid snapshots in the middle of tool calls.
acceptance_criteria: Watcher waits for boundary signal; timeout behavior is configurable; snapshots include turn start/end metadata.

title: Manage background daemon lifecycle
description: Introduce or harden `osixd` responsibilities for mounts, watches, background pushes, cache cleanup, and status reporting.
acceptance_criteria: Long-running watch/mount operations survive normal CLI exits; status and shutdown are available; stale state is recoverable.

title: M8: Agent state policies and side-effect ledger
description: Make OSIx snapshots agent-aware by enforcing state boundaries, side-effect recording, replay safety, and secret scanning.
acceptance_criteria: Secrets are blocked, side-effect ledgers validate, and restored/forked runtimes default external tools to mock, read-only, or approval-required.

title: Implement standard `/agent` state root policy
description: Classify workspace, memory, skills, runtime, logs, state, side-effects, secrets, cache, and tmp paths according to the spec.
acceptance_criteria: Default include/exclude behavior matches the spec; policy can be inspected; policy applies before layer creation.

title: Define and validate side-effect ledger schema
description: Implement `/agent/side-effects/ledger.jsonl` schema validation for tool, turn, idempotency key, request/response digests, external resource, replay policy, and compensating action.
acceptance_criteria: Valid JSONL passes; malformed entries block or warn according to policy; schema examples are documented.

title: Add restore-time replay safety markers
description: Mark restored or forked runtimes so external tool adapters default to mock, read-only, approval-required, or never-replay according to ledger policy.
acceptance_criteria: Restore writes replay mode metadata; tool integrations can read it; docs state default behavior after restore/fork.

title: Integrate secret scanning and redaction hooks
description: Add `--secret-scan block|warn|off`, default deny paths, and log redaction hooks before snapshot layer creation.
acceptance_criteria: Likely secrets block pushed snapshots by default; deny paths are never included; redaction hooks can be configured for logs.

title: M9: Compaction and retention
description: Keep long-running snapshot chains manageable with checkpoints, retention policy, and audit-safe deletion planning.
acceptance_criteria: A delta chain can be compacted into a checkpoint; restore before and after compaction produces the same filesystem state; signed or retained snapshots are preserved.

title: Create checkpoint snapshots
description: Squash a base plus delta chain into a checkpoint layer/manifest while preserving required metadata.
acceptance_criteria: Checkpoint restore matches original chain restore; parent/base metadata is accurate; checkpoint media and annotations validate.

title: Implement retention policy parser
description: Parse hourly, daily, weekly, squash interval, keep snapshots, and preserve-signed retention options.
acceptance_criteria: Policy parser has tests for valid and invalid inputs; dry-run can explain which snapshots are kept or compacted.

title: Implement `osix compact`
description: Apply compaction policy, publish checkpoint manifests, and optionally prune eligible old blobs/manifests according to registry behavior.
acceptance_criteria: `--dry-run` explains planned changes; signed and retained snapshots are not deleted; compaction is resumable after failures.

title: Add audit-safe deletion planning
description: Model which manifests and blobs are safe to delete given signatures, retention, legal hold, branch refs, and external references.
acceptance_criteria: Deletion plan lists reasons for keep/delete; no retained signed snapshot is pruned; docs describe registry GC limits.

title: M10: Registry compatibility matrix and v0 release
description: Validate the end-to-end product against selected registries, document limitations, and prepare v0 release artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: End-to-end flow works for init, mount, run, snapshot, restore, fork, diff, and compact; hybrid and image fallback modes are documented and tested.

title: Build registry compatibility test matrix
description: Test selected registries for blobs, manifests, custom media types, tags, referrers, auth, retention, and fallback behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Matrix identifies supported modes per registry; failures have documented workarounds or explicit v0 limitations.

title: Write end-to-end user documentation
description: Document installation, init, mount, run, snapshot, watch, restore, fork, diff, compact, encryption, signing, and policy workflows.
acceptance_criteria: Docs include a complete CLI session; examples align with implemented flags; limitations and safety defaults are visible.

title: Write threat model and limitations document
description: Document confidentiality, integrity, provenance, replay safety, secret hygiene, tag atomicity, lazy encrypted reads, and registry trust assumptions.
acceptance_criteria: Threat model covers attacker goals and trust boundaries; known limitations are explicit; v0 mitigations are mapped to risks.

title: Prepare v0 release artifacts
description: Package binaries, checksums, release notes, examples, and compatibility notes for the first usable OSIx release.
acceptance_criteria: Release artifacts are reproducible; checksums are published; release notes list features, limitations, and migration expectations.
